ddls:
CREATE TABLE "seismic_events" (
  "event_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each seismic event */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Time when the event occurred */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the event's epicenter */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the event's epicenter */,
  "magnitude" REAL /* Magnitude of the seismic event */,
  "along_strike_distance" REAL /* Distance along the strike from the event's epicenter */,
  "depth" REAL /* Depth of the earthquake epicenter */,
  "focal_mechanism" TEXT /* Information about the fault plane and the direction of slip */,
  "source_mechanism" TEXT /* Details about the source of the earthquake */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of seismic event (e.g., earthquake, explosion, quarry blast) */,
  "intensity" INTEGER /* The intensity of the earthquake as measured on the Modified Mercalli Intensity (MMI) scale */,
  "duration" REAL /* The duration of the seismic event */,
  "quality_factor" REAL /* A measure of the data quality for the event */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* The source of the data (e.g., sensor, satellite, manual input) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the location associated with the event */,
  "magnitude_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the magnitude entry associated with the event */,
  "distance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the distance entry associated with the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_seismic_events_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES event_locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_seismic_events_distance_id FOREIGN KEY ("distance_id") REFERENCES event_distances ("distance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_seismic_events_magnitude_id FOREIGN KEY ("magnitude_id") REFERENCES event_magnitudes ("magnitude_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographical region */,
  "country" TEXT /* The country where the location is situated */,
  "city" TEXT /* The city or town where the location is situated */,
  "population" INTEGER /* The population of the nearest city or town */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* The time zone of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* The elevation of the location */,
  "geological_formation" TEXT /* The geological formation of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_magnitudes" (
  "magnitude_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each magnitude entry */,
  "magnitude_value" REAL /* Value of the magnitude */,
  "magnitude_type" TEXT /* Type of magnitude measurement */,
  "measurement_method" TEXT /* The method used to measure the magnitude */,
  "measurement_location" TEXT /* The location where the magnitude was measured */,
  "measurement_time" TEXT /* The time when the magnitude was measured */,
  "measurement_accuracy" REAL /* The accuracy of the magnitude measurement */,
  "measurement_source" TEXT /* The source of the magnitude measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("magnitude_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_distances" (
  "distance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each distance entry */,
  "event_id" TEXT /* Identifier of the seismic event */,
  "along_strike_distance" REAL /* Distance along the strike from the event's epicenter */,
  "measurement_method" TEXT /* The method used to measure the along-strike distance */,
  "measurement_time" TEXT /* The time when the along-strike distance was measured */,
  "measurement_accuracy" REAL /* The accuracy of the along-strike distance measurement */,
  "measurement_source" TEXT /* The source of the along-strike distance measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., scientist, researcher, emergency responder) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* The phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* The physical address of the user */,
  "organization" TEXT /* The organization the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The last time the user logged into the system */,
  "status" TEXT /* The status of the user (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_event_associations" (
  "user_id" INTEGER,
  "event_id" TEXT,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "event_id"),
  FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"),
  FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES seismic_events ("event_id")
)
